title: erica arborea subsp. riojana
intro: Erica arborea subsp. riojana, also known as the Spanish heath or Rioja heath, is a subspecies of Erica arborea, a species of flowering plant in the heath family. It is a shrub that is native to the Iberian Peninsula, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been shown to have health benefits such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Erica arborea subsp. riojana can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
